channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Major crafts retailer closing all stores
item.description: Less than two weeks after revealing it would close more than 500 stores, craft retailer JOANN says "going-out-of-business sales" will begin at all of its locations. JOANN filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in January, marking the second such filing by the company in less than a year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: COXSACKIE, N.Y. (AP) — A wildcat strike by guards at New York's prisons, now in its second week, is fueling fears over deteriorating conditions behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, CEDAR ATTANASIO and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Police: Holland mom said she killed son to fulfill his death wish
item.description: A mom accused of stabbing her son to death told police she was helping him fulfill his wish: to die before his 18th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Delta flight forced to return to Atlanta airport after 'haze' fills cabin
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight was forced to return to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport on Monday after a "haze" filled the cabin, the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coast Guard detains 2 undocumented immigrants off Massachusetts coast
item.description: During a routine boat inspection on Thursday, the Coast Guard learned two of the six crew members were not U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Roberta Flack, Grammy-winning ‘Killing Me Softly’ singer with an intimate style, dies at 88
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Roberta Flack, the Grammy-winning singer and pianist whose intimate vocal and musical style made her one of the top recordings artists of the 1970s and an influential performer long after, died Monday. She was 88.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump flag in Massachusetts police station gym divides town as chief, official feud over its removal: report
item.description: A Massachusetts police department chief has filed a complaint against a town administrator after he allegedly ordered the police chief to remove a Trump flag from a private gym.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi lands safely in Rome after security concern
item.description: ROME (AP) — An American Airlines flight from New York to New Delhi landed safely in Rome on Sunday afternoon after it was diverted due to a security concern, which later proved to be “non-credible,” the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hochul says she made a case to Trump for NYC's congestion pricing, yet predicts courts will decide
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul says she told President Donald Trump in a private meeting at the White House that congestion pricing tolls in New York City are necessary and working, yet the Democrat predicted the courts will likely decide the matter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, ABC 4

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Idaho murders case: Bryan Kohberger’s likelihood of firing squad execution if convicted increases
item.description: A bill to make death by firing squad the main method of execution in Idaho is moving to the Senate floor for a vote as Bryan Kohberger's trial approaches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wealthy real estate brothers face new lawsuits as defense claims criminal investigation was orchestrated
item.description: New lawsuits against the Alexander brothers were filed, as the brothers claim a high-profile personal-injury law firm "dictated" the criminal investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California governor asks Congress for nearly $40 billion for Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has asked Congress to approve nearly $40 billion in aid to help the Los Angeles area recover from January's devastating wildfires, which he said could become the costliest natural disaster in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group was buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery in Minnesota. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar favorite ‘Anora’ wins best film, director and actor at the Independent Spirit Awards
item.description: Sean Baker's “Anora” won best film, best director and best actor for Mikey Madison at the Independent Spirit Awards on Saturday in what could be a preview of next Sunday’s Oscars: The film about a Brooklyn sex worker and her whirlwind affair with a Russian oligarch’s son has emerged in recent weeks
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Woman accused of drugging and robbing older men in a deadly romance scheme
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A woman used online dating apps to lure at least four older men to meet her in person, then drugged them with sedatives and stole hundreds of thousands of dollars in a “sinister” romance scheme, FBI officials in Las Vegas said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL, JESSE BEDAYN and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former federal worker shares frustration on TikTok after losing job in less than 2 months
item.description: A federal worker who lost her job after being in her position for less than two months hopped on TikTok to tell her story as well as share information and resources to help other federal workers in the same position.
item.dc:creator: Ya-Marie Sesay, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge in Manhattan on Friday extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: 11-year-old dies by suicide after alleged bullying over family’s immigration status
item.description: An 11-year-old Texas girl died by suicide after reportedly being bullied at school over her family's immigration status, according to media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Meza, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday refused to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
